title: Billsboro Winery Celebrates 20 Years of Winemaking in Geneva
summary:
FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE: March 9, 2026 Family-owned winery reflects on two decades of dry wines, community, and sustainability in the Finger Lakes  Geneva, NY – This year, Billsboro Winery celebrates its 20th anniversary under the ownership of Kim and Vinny Aliperti, marking two decades of producing small-batch, dry wines in the Finger Lakes.  After leaving […]

The post Billsboro Winery Celebrates 20 Years of Winemaking in Geneva appeared first on Finger Lakes Wine Alliance.
content:
FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE: March 9, 2026

Family-owned winery reflects on two decades of dry wines, community, and sustainability in the Finger Lakes 

Geneva, NY – This year, Billsboro Winery celebrates its 20th anniversary under the ownership of Kim and Vinny Aliperti, marking two decades of producing small-batch, dry wines in the Finger Lakes. 

After leaving Long Island’s wine country to move to the Finger Lakes in 2000, the Alipertis began the next chapter of their winemaking careers, first at Hermann J. Wiemer Vineyard and later at Atwater Estate Vineyards. In 2006, the opportunity to start their own venture finally became a reality. 

“When Jen called with the offer, we were both a bit flabbergasted,” recalls Kim. “But we quickly realized it was an opportunity we couldn’t pass up.” 

Jennifer Morris and Dr. Bob Pool, a Cornell viticultural researcher, founded the original Billsboro Winery in 1999—Geneva’s very first bonded winery—and were looking to pass the baton. 

What began as a small operation producing 1,000 cases has grown steadily over the past two decades. Today, production has more than quadrupled. Unlike many wineries, Billsboro’s model has always relied entirely on purchasing locally grown grapes. Over the years, the winery has developed strong partnerships with some of the region’s top vineyards while continuing to cultivate new relationships to diversify its grape sourcing. 

“We’ve been incredibly fortunate to work with many dedicated, sustainably minded grape growers,” says Vinny. “They’re the real heroes in our industry,” who often playfully chides his fellow winemakers for getting most of the media attention. 

From the beginning, Billsboro focused on producing relatively small batches of dry wines, many bottled from single-vineyard sources.. That vision continues today, though the winery now works with a larger network of growers and offers a broader range of wines. 

“Twenty years ago, leaning toward an almost entirely dry wine portfolio felt a bit risky,” says Kim, “because we didn’t want to alienate customers who preferred sweeter wines.” 

The winery’s tasting room—housed in a late 19th-century barn—was in turnkey condition when the Alipertis opened to the public in the summer of 2007. Since then, they have remained committed to running the business with environmental sustainability in mind. Initiatives include operating on 100% solar power, maintaining a no-plastic policy, using paper-free restrooms, and composting all eventware. 

Today, the Billsboro farm and its iconic barn reflect both the region’s rich agricultural history and its thriving tourism and hospitality industry. “We are keenly aware of our responsibility to steward the land and the watershed,” says Kim. “We want to ensure that future generations have the same opportunities we were fortunate to receive.”

Get to know the winery

  • March 18, 2026

The post Billsboro Winery Celebrates 20 Years of Winemaking in Geneva appeared first on Finger Lakes Wine Alliance.

title: Weis Vineyards Announces Acquisition of Caywood Vineyards, Renaming Historic Site Weis-Fournier Vineyard
summary:
FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE Weis Vineyards Announces Acquisition of Caywood Vineyards, Renaming Historic Site Weis-Fournier Vineyard Hammondsport, NY – January 2026 – Weis Vineyards is excited to announce the acquisition of the former Caywood Vineyards, marking a significant milestone in the winery’s continued growth and long-term commitment to the Finger Lakes wine region. The historic property […]

The post Weis Vineyards Announces Acquisition of Caywood Vineyards, Renaming Historic Site Weis-Fournier Vineyard appeared first on Finger Lakes Wine Alliance.
content:
FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE
Weis Vineyards Announces Acquisition of Caywood Vineyards,
Renaming Historic Site Weis-Fournier Vineyard
Hammondsport, NY – January 2026 – Weis Vineyards is excited to announce the acquisition of
the former Caywood Vineyards, marking a significant milestone in the winery’s continued
growth and long-term commitment to the Finger Lakes wine region. The historic property will
now be known as Weis-Fournier Vineyard. Located on the east side of Seneca Lake, the vineyard encompasses 182 acres total, with 141 acres currently planted. Plantings at the site began in the early 1970s under the direction of Charles Fournier and have been meticulously and sustainably maintained in
recent years by the Doyle family. Today, the vineyard stands as one of the largest contiguous vinifera plantings on the East Coast featuring mature vines that have contributed to some of the best wines
produced in the Finger Lakes over several decades.
The acquisition enables Weis Vineyards to expand its estate wine program while also preserving
the vineyard’s long-standing role as a premier grape source for winemakers throughout the
region. Weis Vineyards plans to continue supplying high-quality fruit to fellow producers,
reinforcing collaboration and shared excellence within the Finger Lakes wine community.
“We couldn’t be more excited for the opportunity to dive into this next chapter, and we’re so
thankful to be able to share that with the many people who have supported us along the way”
said Peter and Ashlee Weis. “We are honored to carry forward the legacy established by Charles
Fournier and thoughtfully stewarded by the Doyle family.”
Weis-Fournier Vineyard will play a central role in the winery’s ongoing focus on terroir-driven,
sustainably farmed vinifera grapes and reflects Weis Vineyards’ long-term vision for

The post Weis Vineyards Announces Acquisition of Caywood Vineyards, Renaming Historic Site Weis-Fournier Vineyard appeared first on Finger Lakes Wine Alliance.

title: Knapp Winery: Four Decades of Finger Lakes Winemaking
summary:
Founded more than four decades ago, Knapp Winery is one of the Finger Lakes’ most established estates, with deep roots in the region’s modern wine history. Under founders Doug and Susan Knapp, the winery helped shape the Finger Lakes’ identity by championing early vinifera plantings and opening the first winery restaurant on Cayuga Lake. Today, […]

The post Knapp Winery: Four Decades of Finger Lakes Winemaking appeared first on Finger Lakes Wine Alliance.
content:
Founded more than four decades ago, Knapp Winery is one of the Finger Lakes’ most established estates, with deep roots in the region’s modern wine history. Under founders Doug and Susan Knapp, the winery helped shape the Finger Lakes’ identity by championing early vinifera plantings and opening the first winery restaurant on Cayuga Lake. Today, under the leadership of owners Cole and Karen Wilson and winemaker Vanessa Hoffman, Knapp is experiencing a renewed chapter—honoring its legacy while investing in sustainability, innovation, and expressive cool-climate wines that reflect both place and progress.

To learn more, explore Knapp Winery’s feature in Cuvée, the official magazine of the Chaîne des Rôtisseurs, where their story is highlighted in depth.

Facebook
LinkedIn
Instagram

The post Knapp Winery: Four Decades of Finger Lakes Winemaking appeared first on Finger Lakes Wine Alliance.

title: The Finger Lakes has been named Wine Enthusiast’s 2025 American Wine Region of the Year
summary:
[Geneva, NY]  October 16, 2025 — The Finger Lakes has been named Wine Enthusiast’s 2025 American Wine Region of the Year, honoring two centuries of innovation, sustainability, and cool-climate leadership.  This recognition celebrates the winemakers, growers, and partners who have shaped the Finger Lakes into one of America’s most respected wine regions. For the Finger […]

The post The Finger Lakes has been named Wine Enthusiast’s 2025 American Wine Region of the Year  appeared first on Finger Lakes Wine Alliance.
content:
[Geneva, NY]  October 16, 2025 — The Finger Lakes has been named Wine Enthusiast’s 2025 American Wine Region of the Year, honoring two centuries of innovation, sustainability, and cool-climate leadership. 

This recognition celebrates the winemakers, growers, and partners who have shaped the Finger Lakes into one of America’s most respected wine regions. For the Finger Lakes Wine Alliance (FLWA) and its 35+ member wineries, working in collaboration with the New York Wine & Grape Foundation (NYWGF), the award underscores the region’s growing national visibility and the shared momentum behind its rise as a premier cool-climate destination. The region’s wine trails- with the Cayuga Lake Wine Trail, America’s oldest, and those on Keuka and Seneca have long connected visitors to the diversity of Finger Lakes terroir and hospitality, helping shape the region’s identity and experience.

Carved by glaciers and sustained by deep lakes, the Finger Lakes spans over 10,000 vineyard acres where eleven lakes create ideal conditions for slow, balanced ripening. The region’s legacy in sparkling wine production endures alongside its acclaimed Riesling, Chardonnay, Cabernet Franc, and expressive heirloom varieties that reflect both heritage and innovation.

“The Finger Lakes stands as a testament to resilience and collaboration, where generations of growers and new wineries work together to craft exceptional cool-climate wines,” said Amy Navor, Executive Director of the Finger Lakes Wine Alliance. “This recognition from Wine Enthusiast highlights our community’s shared success and innovation, proving that when the Finger Lakes grows, the entire New York wine industry grows. We’re honored to work alongside the New York Wine & Grape Foundation and our peers to showcase the region’s authentic story to the world.”

The award is expected to amplify awareness for the Finger Lakes AVA, driving renewed attention from national trade, media, and travelers. For Alliance members, it opens the door to expanded marketing opportunities and stronger engagement with distributors, sommeliers, and consumers seeking authentic, site-driven wines.

“This honor from Wine Enthusiast celebrates not only the Finger Lakes, but the strength and evolution of New York’s entire wine community,” said Sam Filler, Executive Director of the New York Wine & Grape Foundation. “The American Wine Region of the Year title affirms what generations of growers and winemakers have known: the Finger Lakes is an essential wine region. With a foundation built on two centuries of perseverance, innovation, and pride of place, the Finger Lakes continue to define what cool-climate excellence looks like in America.”

The Wine Enthusiast Wine Star Awards Gala will take place on January 26, 2026, at The Glasshouse in New York City, celebrating excellence, innovation, and community across the global wine industry.

###

About the Finger Lakes Wine Alliance 

The Finger Lakes Wine Alliance (FLWA) is the regional organization dedicated to elevating the visibility and prominence of the Finger Lakes American Viticultural Area (AVA), its wines, and its wineries. Established in 2004, the Alliance unites more than 35 member wineries through trade engagement, education, marketing, and strategic partnerships that strengthen the region’s voice and amplify the people, wines, and vision shaping one of America’s leading cool-climate wine regions. For more information, visit fingerlakeswinealliance.com.

​​About the New York Wine & Grape Foundation  

The mission of the New York Wine & Grape Foundation is to promote the world-class image of New York grapes and wines from our diverse regions to responsibly benefit farmers, producers, and consumers through innovative marketing, research, communication, and advocacy. Learn more at newyorkwines.org. 

CONTACT: 
Finger Lakes Wine Alliance 
Amy Navor, Executive Director 
‪(315) 563-1480‬
amy@fingerlakeswinealliance.com 

The post The Finger Lakes has been named Wine Enthusiast’s 2025 American Wine Region of the Year  appeared first on Finger Lakes Wine Alliance.

title: FRIDAY CONVERSATION WITH … Nova Cadamatre

Cadamatre, also founder of Trestle Thirty One, will mentor the Heron Hill team while helping preserve the […]

The post FRIDAY CONVERSATION WITH … Nova Cadamatre appeared first on Finger Lakes Wine Alliance.
content:
Heron Hill Winery in Urbana is entering a new chapter with a refreshed winemaking team, including Nova Cadamatre, the first female winemaker in the U.S. to earn the prestigious Master of Wine title, who joins as consulting winemaker. Cadamatre, also founder of Trestle Thirty One, will mentor the Heron Hill team while helping preserve the winery’s history and introduce new innovations to the Finger Lakes wine scene.

content:
Downtown Geneva has become a hub for sommeliers and wine pros, with wineries like Trestle Thirty One creating a dynamic scene that highlights Finger Lakes wines alongside global selections. Master of Wine Nova Cadamatre leads Trestle Thirty One with a focus on small-batch, terroir-driven wines—most recently experimenting with a dry Riesling aged in acacia barrels—showcasing the region’s innovation and high standards in hospitality.

summary:

Standouts like Boundary Breaks’ acclaimed Dry Riesling, Lamoreaux Landing’s award-winning Cabernet Franc, and Atwater’s funky low-intervention wines round out an unforgettable tasting journey. title: FLX Chamber Hosts 25th Anniversary for Ravines Wine Cellars
summary:
The Finger Lakes Chamber of Commerce honored Ravines Wine Cellars’ 25th anniversary with a Member Milestone Celebration at their Geneva location on August 14, featuring tours, Q&A with the owners, and a toast with their 2019 Sparkling Limestone Springs Riesling. Founded in 2000 by Morten and Lisa Hallgren, Ravines has grown into a celebrated Finger […]

The post FLX Chamber Hosts 25th Anniversary for Ravines Wine Cellars appeared first on Finger Lakes Wine Alliance.
content:
The Finger Lakes Chamber of Commerce honored Ravines Wine Cellars’ 25th anniversary with a Member Milestone Celebration at their Geneva location on August 14, featuring tours, Q&A with the owners, and a toast with their 2019 Sparkling Limestone Springs Riesling. Founded in 2000 by Morten and Lisa Hallgren, Ravines has grown into a celebrated Finger Lakes winery with two tasting rooms, 130+ acres of estate vineyards, and national recognition for its distinctive dry Rieslings.

Click here for the full story!

content:
[Geneva, NY]  October 16, 2025 — The Finger Lakes has been named Wine Enthusiast’s 2025 American Wine Region of the Year, honoring two centuries of innovation, sustainability, and cool-climate leadership. 

This recognition celebrates the winemakers, growers, and partners who have shaped the Finger Lakes into one of America’s most respected wine regions. Carved by glaciers and sustained by deep lakes, the Finger Lakes spans over 10,000 vineyard acres where eleven lakes create ideal conditions for slow, balanced ripening. The region’s legacy in sparkling wine production endures alongside its acclaimed Riesling, Chardonnay, Cabernet Franc, and expressive heirloom varieties that reflect both heritage and innovation.

“The Finger Lakes stands as a testament to resilience and collaboration, where generations of growers and new wineries work together to craft exceptional cool-climate wines,” said Amy Navor, Executive Director of the Finger Lakes Wine Alliance. “This recognition from Wine Enthusiast highlights our community’s shared success and innovation, proving that when the Finger Lakes grows, the entire New York wine industry grows. We’re honored to work alongside the New York Wine & Grape Foundation and our peers to showcase the region’s authentic story to the world.”

The award is expected to amplify awareness for the Finger Lakes AVA, driving renewed attention from national trade, media, and travelers. For Alliance members, it opens the door to expanded marketing opportunities and stronger engagement with distributors, sommeliers, and consumers seeking authentic, site-driven wines.

“This honor from Wine Enthusiast celebrates not only the Finger Lakes, but the strength and evolution of New York’s entire wine community,” said Sam Filler, Executive Director of the New York Wine & Grape Foundation. “The American Wine Region of the Year title affirms what generations of growers and winemakers have known: the Finger Lakes is an essential wine region. With a foundation built on two centuries of perseverance, innovation, and pride of place, the Finger Lakes continue to define what cool-climate excellence looks like in America.”

The Wine Enthusiast Wine Star Awards Gala will take place on January 26, 2026, at The Glasshouse in New York City, celebrating excellence, innovation, and community across the global wine industry.

About the Finger Lakes Wine Alliance 

The Finger Lakes Wine Alliance (FLWA) is the regional organization dedicated to elevating the visibility and prominence of the Finger Lakes American Viticultural Area (AVA), its wines, and its wineries. Established in 2004, the Alliance unites more than 35 member wineries through trade engagement, education, marketing, and strategic partnerships that strengthen the region’s voice and amplify the people, wines, and vision shaping one of America’s leading cool-climate wine regions. title: Downtown Geneva Has Morphed Into a Hub For Wine Pros
summary:
Downtown Geneva has become a hub for sommeliers and wine pros, with wineries like Trestle Thirty One creating a dynamic scene that highlights Finger Lakes wines alongside global selections. Master of Wine Nova Cadamatre leads Trestle Thirty One with a focus on small-batch, terroir-driven wines—most recently experimenting with a dry Riesling aged in acacia barrels—showcasing […]

The post Downtown Geneva Has Morphed Into a Hub For Wine Pros appeared first on Finger Lakes Wine Alliance.
content:
Downtown Geneva has become a hub for sommeliers and wine pros, with wineries like Trestle Thirty One creating a dynamic scene that highlights Finger Lakes wines alongside global selections. Master of Wine Nova Cadamatre leads Trestle Thirty One with a focus on small-batch, terroir-driven wines—most recently experimenting with a dry Riesling aged in acacia barrels—showcasing the region’s innovation and high standards in hospitality.

Click here to read the full story!

The post Downtown Geneva Has Morphed Into a Hub For Wine Pros appeared first on Finger Lakes Wine Alliance.

title: The Philadelphian’s Travel Guide to New York State
summary:
A tour of Keuka and Seneca Lakes highlights the Finger Lakes’ diversity, from Dr. Konstantin Frank’s trailblazing sparkling wines to Living Roots’ playful cross-hemisphere creations. Standouts like Boundary Breaks’ acclaimed Dry Riesling, Lamoreaux Landing’s award-winning Cabernet Franc, and Atwater’s funky low-intervention wines round out an unforgettable tasting journey. title: FLX Chamber Hosts 25th Anniversary for Ravines Wine Cellars
summary:
The Finger Lakes Chamber of Commerce honored Ravines Wine Cellars’ 25th anniversary with a Member Milestone Celebration at their Geneva location on August 14, featuring tours, Q&A with the owners, and a toast with their 2019 Sparkling Limestone Springs Riesling. Founded in 2000 by Morten and Lisa Hallgren, Ravines has grown into a celebrated Finger […]

The post FLX Chamber Hosts 25th Anniversary for Ravines Wine Cellars appeared first on Finger Lakes Wine Alliance.
content:
The Finger Lakes Chamber of Commerce honored Ravines Wine Cellars’ 25th anniversary with a Member Milestone Celebration at their Geneva location on August 14, featuring tours, Q&A with the owners, and a toast with their 2019 Sparkling Limestone Springs Riesling. Founded in 2000 by Morten and Lisa Hallgren, Ravines has grown into a celebrated Finger Lakes winery with two tasting rooms, 130+ acres of estate vineyards, and national recognition for its distinctive dry Rieslings.

Click here for the full story!

The post FLX Chamber Hosts 25th Anniversary for Ravines Wine Cellars appeared first on Finger Lakes Wine Alliance.
